feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Cat Ownership Linked to Increased Risk of Schizophrenia, Study Suggests
entry.summary: Having a cat as a pet is linked to an increased risk of schizophrenia-related conditions, according to a 2023 analysis of 17 studies.

entry.title: A natural depsipeptide antibiotic binds the E-site of the bacterial ribosome
entry.summary: Improved fractionation strategies can identify antibiotics with previously unseen scaffolds and mechanisms, exemplified by manikomycin from Streptomyces rimosus, which acts by targeting the E-site of the bacterial large ribosomal subunit.

entry.title: Acquired genetic and cell-state changes in IDH-mutant glioma progression
entry.summary: Longitudinal transcriptomic, chromatin and genomic analyses of the two types of IDH-mutant glioma reveal in detail how disease progression is influenced by interdependent genetic, epigenetic and microenvironmental factors.

entry.title: Google wants to release 64 million bacteria-riddled mosquitoes across California and Florida. Here’s why scientists are enthusiastic.
entry.summary: Google has applied for an experimental mosquito release permit to deploy millions of non-biting southern house mosquitoes that it has infected with the bacterium Wolbachia pipientis, in an effort to reduce mosquito-borne diseases like West Nile virus.

entry.title: Human Rabies Exposure Reported As 1st Bat Tests Positive In McHenry County
entry.summary: Health officials say a resident received preventive treatment after finding a bat inside a home and are urging residents to take precautions

entry.title: 4 Common Vegetables With More Fiber Than Broccoli
entry.summary: Broccoli contains a moderate amount of fiber, but other vegetables like green peas and artichokes have higher fiber content.

entry.title: The Worst Health Habits You're Probably Picking Up In Middle Age
entry.summary: “The budgeting of time to take care of your physical and mental health actually becomes even more important at this stage of life, but a lot of people do not realize that until symptoms start showing up.”

entry.title: Scientists Say These Vitamin Deficiencies Could Be Causing Your Fatigue
entry.summary: New research breaks down how low levels of B12 and folate can lead to decreased energy levels.

entry.title: The Best Exercise Combination for Longevity, According to a 30-Year Study
entry.summary: New research indicates that a moderate amount of weekly strength training may be associated with the greatest longevity benefits, especially when paired with regular aerobic exercise.

entry.title: Consensus meta-analysis of genome-wide association studies for Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias
entry.summary: An updated meta-analysis of genome-wide association studies identified 91 risk loci for Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and related dementias among individuals of European ancestry, of which 56 loci were specifically detected in clinically diagnosed AD cases.

entry.title: 'I'm 60 and still getting stronger – these are the 11 exercises I repeat every week for full-body strength'
entry.summary: Fitness coach Suzi Jalowsky shares the simple 30-minute full-body workout she does three times a week to stay strong, mobile and active at 60.

entry.title: On Statins or Beta-Blockers? Watch for These Heart Medication Side Effects
entry.summary: If you're one of the millions of women taking medication to protect your heart, you already know how much these prescriptions can do for your health. But her...

entry.title: Sin Nombre Hantavirus Strain Kills One In Arizona
entry.summary: Everything to know about the Sin Nombre strain of Hantavirus, from mortality rate to how it spreads.

entry.title: This new diabetes pill burns fat without the downsides of Ozempic
entry.summary: Scientists have developed an experimental diabetes and obesity pill that works in a completely different way from drugs like Ozempic. Rather than reducing hunger, it activates metabolism in skeletal muscle, helping lower blood sugar and increase fat burning w…

entry.title: 'It's still working': More are living with cancer as era of targeted drugs takes hold
entry.summary: By Deena Beasley June 3 (Reuters) - Cathy Smithwick, now 67, has lived with breast cancer, and then ovarian cancer, for more than 20 years with the help of t...

entry.title: ADHD med made 3-year-old psychotic
entry.summary: In this edition of The People’s Pharmacy, Joe and Teresa Graedon also hear from a reader who wants to know more about Voltaren Gel.

entry.title: What your hair color might tell you about your health
entry.summary: Research shows that pain threshold, melanoma risk and other conditions could be linked to the color of your hair.

entry.title: Interest in a Potentially Toxic Measles Treatment Spiked After Joe Rogan Bump, Study Says
entry.summary: Comments by RFK Jr. and Joe Rogan appear to have encouraged a 38.7% increase in vitamin A poisoning cases early last year.

entry.title: 2 scientists charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into the US and lying to authorities
entry.summary: Two scientists at a U.S. government lab were charged with smuggling vials of deactivated mpox virus into the country from Africa and lying about it during interviews with investigators at a Michigan airport, authorities said.

entry.title: Experts Share How Much Water to Drink Daily to Prevent Kidney Stones
entry.summary: Experts share tips to stay hydrated and reduce your risk.

entry.title: This Miserable Illness Just Peaked Nationwide — And Many People Mistake It For Seasonal Allergies
entry.summary: As kids and adults head outside to enjoy warmer weather, cases typically decrease — but that doesn't mean it's totally gone.

entry.title: The science around GLP-1 drugs and cancer is suddenly getting a lot more interesting
entry.summary: Studies released in recent weeks are fueling optimism — and fresh questions — about GLP-1 drugs' potential role in cancer prevention and treatment.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: Doctors couldn't explain why a boy was bleeding from his eyes, ears and nose
entry.summary: A case of a boy who bled from his eyes eventually led doctors to a diagnosis that has been reported fewer than 50 times in the medical literature.

entry.title: How to know if your pet has allergies
entry.summary: Cats and dogs can be allergic to pollen, but getting a diagnosis is a lengthy process.

entry.title: Worrying COVID ‘Cicada’ Variant Spreads as US Maps Go Dark
entry.summary: Cuts to one of America's major disease surveillance programs could threaten public health, experts say, as COVID 'cicada variant spreads.

entry.title: A science powerhouse bets on genetic therapy to beat brain disorders
entry.summary: The Allen Institute in Seattle says scientists have now learned enough about how the brain works to start fixing it when it breaks.

entry.title: 6 Best Foods to Eat If You Have IBS, Recommended by Dietitians
entry.summary: Gut health nutrition experts share the top foods they recommend to patients who struggle with IBS, with a focus on whole, nutrient-dense foods.

entry.title: Can the rush for vaccines slow the latest Ebola outbreak?
entry.summary: The Bundibugyo strain is a reminder that serious health threats can escalate from under-the-radar pathogens

entry.title: 2 researchers charged with smuggling mpox into the US
entry.summary: Employed at a National Institutes of Health lab in Montana, the two allegedly brought deactivated virus from the Republic of the Congo without a permit.

entry.title: 4 Nighttime Habits You Need to Start to Help Slow Aging, According to Doctors
entry.summary: Doctors share tips that can support healthy aging by improving sleep, circadian rhythm and metabolic health.

entry.title: The Cancer Research Machine Trump Is Gutting Just Delivered a Big Breakthrough
entry.summary: There’s new hope for one of the deadliest cancers. But Trump’s war on research puts future discoveries at risk.

entry.title: Senior NIH scientist, research fellow charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into U.S.
entry.summary: A senior NIH scientist and his research fellow were charged with smuggling vials of deactivated mpox virus into the country from Africa and lying about it.

entry.title: Five people have left hantavirus quarantine facility in Nebraska
entry.summary: Some passengers from a hantavirus-striken cruise ship have returned to their home states, where they are under the watch of public health authorities for the remainder of their monitoring period.

entry.title: It’s a fight for two lives: a mother is trying to survive for her unborn daughter
entry.summary: IDAHO FALLS — A local husband and his family are facing a situation they never imagined. He is fighting for his wife’s life while also trying to protect their unborn baby. They additionally have to relocate to another state on short notice for necessary care …

entry.title: Woman Says Doctor Told Her to Cancel a Scan. Then She Was Diagnosed with Stage 4 Cancer
entry.summary: "If something doesn’t feel right with your health, it probably isn’t," said Clare Din

entry.title: Bang! Exploding immune cells splatter potent toxins everywhere
entry.summary: Detonation of newly discovered ‘ruptoblasts’, found in flatworms, releases compounds that kill nearby cells in minutes.

entry.title: Single cell transforms into cannibalistic 'supergiant,' swallowing its clones whole
entry.summary: Researchers at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI) have discovered a microscopic organism that can transform into a cannibalistic "supergiant" that drastically changes size, shape, and behavior, and abandons filter-feeding to hunt and consume their genetic…

entry.title: Largest study of knee osteoarthritis tissue reveals core biological pathways underlying the disease
entry.summary: A major international study led by researchers at the Kennedy Institute of Rheumatology at the University of Oxford has found that osteoarthritis (OA)—the most common form of arthritis worldwide—is not a collection of separate diseases, as many scientists had…

entry.title: “Once It’s Gone, It’s Gone”: The Silent Brain-Sapper Striking Millions of Postmenopausal Women
entry.summary: A new study has linked postmenopausal hormone changes to an irreversible disease that plagues 7.4 million Americans over 65.

entry.title: 5 Best Anti-Inflammatory Carbs You Should Be Eating, According to a Dietitian
entry.summary: You may be surprised to hear that carbs can be anti-inflammatory. Learn what 5 of the best anti-inflammatory carbs are that dietitians want you to eat.

entry.title: Why Google wants to release millions of mosquitoes in the US
entry.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

entry.title: Number of suspected Ebola cases falls by hundreds as testing ramps up
entry.summary: The number of cases falls from 1,100 to 437 with increased testing.

entry.title: Even One Drink a Day Could Raise Your Cancer Risk, Study Warns
entry.summary: For some health conditions like cancer, there's no safe level of alcohol consumption, a large new review finds.

entry.title: 4 Standing Exercises That Shrink Belly Overhang Faster Than Gym Workouts After 55
entry.summary: A CPT shares 4 standing exercises that strengthen the core and address belly overhang after 55.

entry.title: Can marriage improve your health? Here are the benefits that experts see.
entry.summary: Saying “I do” may influence your cancer risk, mental health and more.

entry.title: Google wants to set 64 million mosquitoes loose in California and Florida
entry.summary: The company’s ‘Debugging’ division hopes to reduce insect-borne illnesses by releasing millions of sterile males into the wild

entry.title: Doctors May Need To Rethink Calcium and Vitamin D Recommendations After Major Review
entry.summary: New evidence suggests calcium and vitamin D supplements may do far less to prevent fractures and falls than widely believed.

entry.title: Get to know your TRICARE health plan with the TRICARE Plans Overview Fact Sheet
entry.summary: Learn about your TRICARE health plan today with a simple tool that makes it easy. The TRICARE Plans Overview Fact Sheet is your go-to resource for understanding what you and your family members can

entry.title: 5 Chair Exercises That Restore Walking Strength Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A CPT shares 5 chair moves that restore walking strength after 60.

entry.title: Researchers Suspected Brain Inflammation in Long COVID but Found Something Else
entry.summary: Brain scans suggest long COVID’s biggest clues may lie in the brain’s emotion centers, not widespread inflammation.
